content: If we map the linguistic definition of "reflexion" (as reflection of light off a surface) onto the LLM iterative refinement framework of Self-Refine, we can reframe LLM self-improvement as a process of iterative light reflection: each new LLM output bounces off the "mirror" of the model's own prior feedback to correct its direction, just as repeated reflection between parallel mirrors amplifies and refines an image rather than just reproducing it.
